question:
Will Newcastle United Jets FC win on 2025-11-09?

Description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for November 9, 2025
If Newcastle United Jets FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: https://www.a-league.com.au/

Mizumuma was not in the squad for the Isuzu UTE A-League Round 1 opener in Gosford after suffering a training-ground injury in the leadup to the season-opening showdown. Mizumuma has endeared himself to not only Jets fans but A-Leagues supporters since moving to Newcastle from J1 League club Yokohama F.Marinos in the second half of 2024-25. evidences.title: Newcastle Jets preview - A-Leagues
evidences.content: “We needed to win one of our last three games to make the finals last season, and if that (scenario) happens again we would all be very disappointed. The club have given us the extra players that we lacked last year, so the responsibility is on us as players and coaches to make the finals. Having said that every team has strengthened and we have a tougher draw than last year, but it is a good challenge and we are looking forward to it.” First match: v Melbourne City, CB Smith Reserve, Melbourne, Sunday 6 November, 3pm First home match: v Perth Glory, McDonald Jones Stadium, Newcastle, Sunday 20 November, 2.30pm (Hyundai A-League double-header – Newcastle Jets v Central Coast Mariners, 5pm) [...] Ins: Liz O’Reilly (Sydney University, NSW), Emma Stanbury (Sydney University, NSW), Jennifer Hoy (Chicago Red Stars, USA), Tara Pender (Western Sydney Wanderers), Liana Danaskos (Sydney University, NSW) Outs: Georgia Yeoman-Dale (Sydney FC), Tara Andrews (year off), Kobie Ferguson (ACL injury), Adriana Jones (Adelaide United), Siahn Bozanic (released), Hannah Bromley (retired), Hannah Southwell (left football) ## News & Updates ### A-League All Stars Women: Yallop the first star named A-...

evidences.title: Preview: Newcastle Jets v Melbourne Heart - A-Leagues
evidences.content: By the end of the season, they were a tough proposition for any opponent, and came close to squeezing into the play-offs. This season, they’ll be absolutely determined to go one better. New signing David Williams will be looking to get amongst the goals, as will Brazilian Maycon Carvalho Inez, while Jonathan Germano will add more South American flair. And then, of course, there’s Helbert Frederico Carreiro da Silva, better known as Fred. The Brazilian has been announced as club captain and Heart supporters will be hoping that the former Victory man can do the business for them in the midfield. [...] The season-ending injury to amrquee man jason Culina and subsequent shock sacking of coach Branko will doubtless have an impact on this game. A shame, as the Jets’ transfer policy in the off-season has been smart and Fury striker Chris Payne adds some much needed fire-power to their attack-line. If they can keep as tight at the back as they were last season, while finding the net with a bit more regularity, Jets are certainly a force to be reckoned with this time round. Melbourne Heart will also be looking to show how far they’ve progressed. This time last year, they made their Hyund...

evidences.title: Newcastle United Injuries and Suspensions News - NewsNow
evidences.content: Suspensions present additional selection challenges, with the Premier League's disciplinary system impacting team availability throughout the season. The FA's retrospective action panel occasionally reviews incidents involving Newcastle players, while yellow card accumulation requires careful management by the coaching staff, particularly for key positions where squad depth may be tested. [...] Throughout its rich history, Newcastle United has evolved its approach to injury prevention and management, investing in modern medical facilities despite periods of financial constraint. The club's medical team utilises contemporary recovery techniques, including cryotherapy chambers and personalised rehabilitation plans. This professional approach reflects the growing importance of sports science in maintaining player availability in the competitive Premier League environment. evidences.title: Newcastle United season preview: Hello despondency, our old friend… - The Athletic
evidences.content: ## What are people excited about? Here, we need to take a huge step back and then lift our heads. Howe is an elite coach. Newcastle have a fantastic team in the most basic sense of the word. They have some outrageously good players. Elanga is exciting. They are in the Champions League again. They have shown repeatedly they can compete with and beat the best teams in the land. They are cup holders. Perhaps most pertinently, they are very, very good at facing down adversity. Anthony Elanga adds even more pace to Newcastle’s play (Han Myung-Gu/Getty Images) ## What are they worried about? [...] ## What are they worried about? Isak, naturally. Their best player, a striker of sublime quality who has scored 20 goals or more in consecutive seasons, wants out. Will he stay? Can he be rehabilitated? If he goes, most likely to Liverpool, what does Newcastle’s team look like? Can they bring in a comparable replacement? Will Newcastle get other business done? Advertisement There is a bigger picture, too, Where are the concrete developments (literally) about a new stadium or training ground? Is the great Newcastle project still moving? Yes, according to Howe. “The owners are very much engage...

evidences.title: Premier League 2025/2026 Stats : Goals, Assists, Cards, Tackles + More
evidences.content: Ultimately, Premier League goalkeeping statistics are most informative when combined with defensive structure and shot volume, helping to separate individual performance from the wider tactical framework. ## Set-Piece Contribution Set-piece data plays a crucial role in understanding how Premier League teams create scoring opportunities beyond open play. Goals from corners, free-kicks, and indirect restarts often prove decisive over the course of a season, particularly in matches where space is limited and chances are hard to come by. [...] Set pieces are especially valuable for mid-table and lower-ranked Premier League teams. When facing opponents with greater control of possession or attacking quality, dead-ball scenarios offer a vital alternative route to goal. In closely fought fixtures, a single well-executed corner or free-kick can be the difference between securing points and coming away empty-handed. For these reasons, set-piece contribution remains a key area of analysis when evaluating how Premier League teams exploit small margins and turn structured moments into decisive advantages. ## How to Use Premier League Stats Together [...] Tracking goals from dead-ball situat...
